questions.a: A word that names a person, place, thing, or idea
A word that describes an action, state, or occurrence
A word that describes or modifies a noun
A word that modifies a verb, adjective, or another adverb (often ends in -ly)
A comparison using 'like' or 'as' (e.g. 'fast as lightning')
A direct comparison saying something IS something else (e.g. 'life is a journey')
The repetition of the same starting sound in nearby words
A word that means the same (or similar) as another word
A word that means the opposite of another word
